serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of stir-fried shrimp with salted egg is approximately 150 g, representing a standard restaurant or home-cooked portion of 6–8 medium shrimp coated in salted egg yolk sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Stir Fried Shrimp with Salted Egg — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g per 100 g) taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for this dish (cr_id G00010578, confidence 0.95). Secondary nutrients estimated from USDA FoodData Central shrimp and salted egg component data where HPB does not report them; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for stir-fried shrimp with salted egg. GI estimated at 40 (low) by analogy with mixed shrimp-and-egg dishes of similar macronutrient profile (very low carbohydrate ~2.4 g/100 g, high protein ~10.7 g/100 g) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (40 × 2.36 × 1.5) / 100 ≈ 1.4 g, confirming negligible glycaemic impact.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~850 mg/100 g) from salted duck egg yolk and added salt, substantially exceeding the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 50–75 g per meal, request reduced-salt preparation, avoid pairing with other high-sodium dishes, and monitor blood pressure response; the salted egg yolk component is the primary sodium driver and should be minimised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (~2.36 g/100 g) and high protein (~10.7 g/100 g) from shrimp, resulting in a glycaemic load of approximately 1.4 per 150 g serving, which has negligible impact on postprandial blood glucose.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is suitable for people with type 2 diabetes in moderate portions; monitor total meal carbohydrate from accompanying rice, be aware that the saturated fat from salted egg yolk may worsen insulin resistance over time if consumed frequently, and choose steamed rice in small quantities as the accompaniment.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (~850 mg/100 g) from salted egg yolk raises blood pressure and cardiovascular risk; saturated fat from egg yolk and butter contributes to LDL elevation; cholesterol content of salted egg yolk is also elevated.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: People with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish only occasionally and in small portions (≤75 g), request preparation with reduced butter and salted egg yolk, avoid pairing with other sodium-dense foods, and prioritise heart-healthy accompaniments such as steamed vegetables.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium (~850 mg/100 g) worsens RA-related tissue oedema; chili is a nightshade that may trigger joint inflammation in sensitive individuals; saturated fat from butter and salted egg yolk is pro-inflammatory.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request chili-free preparation, limit salted egg yolk quantity, and consume only small portions infrequently; the shrimp protein and omega-3 content are beneficial but do not offset the sodium and nightshade concerns.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-Fried Shrimp with Salted Egg (Vietnamese: Tôm Sốt Trứng Muối) is a popular Vietnamese and Vietnamese-Singaporean dish consisting of fresh shrimp stir-fried in a rich, savoury sauce made from mashed salted duck egg yolks, butter, curry leaves, garlic, and chili. The salted egg yolk coating gives the shrimp a golden, slightly foamy, intensely umami crust with a creamy, salty flavour profile and a tender-crisp texture. The dish is commonly served as a main course with steamed jasmine rice at lunch or dinner. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 55 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 0.0 g fat as reported by HPB (fat_g confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source for this specific entry; see notes), and 2.36 g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, low-carbohydrate option. However, sodium content is very high at approximately 850 mg/100 g due to the salted egg yolk, making it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease unless portion-controlled. The glycaemic load per standard 150 g serving is approximately 1.4, confirming negligible impact on blood glucose. Clinically, the dish is low-risk for type 2 diabetes but high-risk for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to sodium and saturated fat load from the salted egg component.

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, carbohydrate) are sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database at 0.95 confidence. Fat is reported as 0.0 g by HPB for this entry. Secondary nutrients (sodium, potassium, sugar, fibre, saturated fat) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for shrimp and salted egg yolk and are not directly measured for this specific dish preparation, reducing overall confidence to medium.
notes: fat_g is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g by the HPB Foodo source (cr_id G00010578, confidence 0.95); this is confirmed zero per the HPB Singapore database for this specific dish entry. saturated_fat_g is set to 0.0 consistent with the HPB-reported fat_g of 0.0; however, this is nutritionally atypical for a dish containing salted egg yolk and butter — the HPB figure may reflect a specific preparation variant or analytical rounding; consumers should be aware that typical preparations of this dish contain meaningful saturated fat. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this specific Vietnamese stir-fried shrimp with salted egg preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish entry as of 2026-04-16. Sodium (~850 mg/100 g), potassium (~220 mg/100 g), sugar (~0.5 g/100 g), and dietary fibre (~0.2 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked shrimp (FDC ID 175180) and salted duck egg yolk and are flagged as estimates. Halal status is marked false as salted egg dishes in Vietnamese restaurants may use non-halal preparation methods; verification required per establishment. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate; no direct measurement exists for this dish.
